cards.title: AI Companies Race Toward Public Markets
cards.body: Startups are attempting to capitalize on the SpaceX IPO wave and go public. The IPO market is heating up with AI-focused companies leading the charge.

cards.title: Anthropic Suspends Model Access Amid India Debate
cards.body: Anthropic has suspended access to new models following government concerns. Tech leaders in India are debating whether this is a wake-up call for the country's AI ambitions.

cards.title: Meta Unwinds $2B Manus Deal After Beijing Pressure
cards.body: Meta is dismantling its $2 billion acquisition of Manus following orders from Beijing. The reversal marks a significant setback for Meta's international AI expansion.

cards.title: KPMG Pulls AI Report Due to Hallucinations
cards.body: KPMG retracted a report on AI usage after discovering apparent hallucinations in the data. The incident highlights ongoing reliability concerns with AI-generated information.

cards.title: Amazon CEO Allegedly Raised Anthropic Security Concerns
cards.body: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy may have been the source of security concerns leading to Anthropic's model restrictions. The concerns prompted Anthropic to cut off worldwide access to two models.

cards.title: OpenAI Faces State Attorneys General Investigation
cards.body: Multiple state attorneys general are investigating OpenAI's practices. Inquiries cover ad policies, health data handling, and other operational concerns.

cards.title: Andrew Yang Eyes Cost-of-Living Startup Opportunity
cards.body: Andrew Yang identifies housing, food, and wireless costs as ripe for disruption. He believes the next startup gold rush will focus on lowering the cost of living.

cards.title: Government Pulls Plug on Anthropic's Powerful AI
cards.body: The government recalled Anthropic's most powerful AI model over a narrow potential jailbreak vulnerability. Anthropic publicly disagreed with the decision, calling it an overreach.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO: Live Coverage and Analysis
cards.body: TechCrunch is providing comprehensive coverage of SpaceX's IPO journey. Coverage includes pre-IPO deals, S-1 documents, and winner analysis.

cards.title: Meta's AI Unit Described as 'Soul-Crushing Gulag'
cards.body: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit is reportedly on the verge of internal revolt. Engineers describe the workplace environment as severely demoralizing.

cards.title: Google Sues Chinese AI-Powered Cybercrime Operation
cards.body: Google sued 'Outsider Enterprise' for using AI to scam hundreds of thousands of victims. The group sent 2.5 million text messages in just two weeks.

cards.title: Mistral Reportedly Raises €3B at €20B Valuation
cards.body: Mistral is in talks to raise €3 billion at a €20 billion valuation. The funding would nearly double the company's Series C valuation of €11.7 billion.

cards.title: MANGOS Replace FAANG in IPO Market Leadership
cards.body: A new generation of companies—Meta, Anthropic, Nvidia, Google, OpenAI, and SpaceX—are leading the IPO charge. The shift signals a major realignment in tech market dominance.

cards.title: Avataar's Video AI Priced for India's Scale
cards.body: Avataar AI released a distilled video model priced at just $0.005 per second of generation. The tool is culturally tuned and designed for India's massive market.

cards.title: Theker Raises $85M for Reconfigurable Factory Robots
cards.body: Theker secured $85 million to build flexible factory robots that don't specialize in one task. Unlike fixed-form humanoids, Theker's machines are built for rapid reconfiguration.

cards.title: Bezos's Prometheus Raises $12B for Physical AI
cards.body: Prometheus, backed by Jeff Bezos, raised $12 billion to build an 'artificial general engineer' for the physical world. The startup is now valued at $41 billion.

cards.title: SpaceX Prices IPO Shares at $135 Per Share
cards.body: SpaceX officially priced its IPO shares at $135, marking the largest IPO ever. The company has begun its public market debut.

cards.title: SpaceX SPV Investors Face Hidden Fees and Delays
cards.body: Lower-tier SpaceX SPV investors won't know their true holdings until post-IPO lock-up periods lift. They face hidden fees, lengthy payment delays, and fraud risks.

cards.title: Deezer Tool Identifies AI-Generated Music Across Platforms
cards.body: Deezer launched a tool that scans Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to detect AI-generated music. The solution addresses growing concerns about synthetic audio infiltrating streaming services.

cards.title: As AI companies race to go public, who else rides the wave?
cards.body: Startups are attempting to capitalize on the IPO momentum similar to SpaceX's public debut. The trend reflects growing investor appetite for AI-focused companies.

cards.title: Anthropic suspends access to new models, India debates AI future
cards.body: India's tech leaders are questioning the implications of Anthropic's model access suspension for the country's AI ambitions. The episode raises concerns about regulatory control over AI development.

cards.title: KPMG pulls report on AI usage due to hallucinations
cards.body: KPMG retracted a report after discovering apparent AI-generated hallucinations in the content. The incident highlights ongoing reliability concerns with AI systems.

cards.title: Amazon CEO raised Anthropic model concerns before crackdown
cards.body: Andy Jassy may have flagged security concerns about Anthropic's models to government officials. This reportedly prompted the company to restrict worldwide access to two models.

cards.title: OpenAI faces investigation from state attorneys general
cards.body: Multiple state AGs are investigating OpenAI's ad policies and health data handling practices. The scope and identity of involved states remain unclear.

cards.title: Anthropic's safety warnings backfire as government pulls the plug
cards.body: The government ordered Anthropic to recall its most powerful AI model following a narrow jailbreak discovery. Anthropic publicly disagreed with the decision, calling it disproportionate.

cards.title: Meta's 6,500-person AI unit described as soul-crushing gulag
cards.body: Engineers inside Meta's AI division reportedly express deep dissatisfaction with workplace conditions. The unit is described as being on the verge of internal revolt.

cards.title: Chinese cybercrime operation used AI to scam hundreds of thousands
cards.body: Google sued a group called 'Outsider Enterprise' for using AI to perpetrate mass fraud. The operation sent 2.5 million text messages over just two weeks.

cards.title: Mistral rumored to raise €3B at €20B valuation
cards.body: The funding round would nearly double Mistral's Series C valuation of €11.7 billion. This positions the AI startup among the industry's most valuable companies.

cards.title: SpaceX, Anthropic, and OpenAI lead IPO summer charge
cards.body: A new wave of IPO leaders is emerging, with the acronym MANGOS replacing FAANG. Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are driving the market shift.

cards.title: Avataar's video AI built for India's scale at ultra-low cost
cards.body: Avataar's distilled video model generates content at just $0.005 per second. The approach prioritizes affordability and cultural awareness for Indian markets.

cards.title: Jeff Bezos' Prometheus raises $12B for artificial general engineer
cards.body: The startup aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design tasks using physical AI. The funding values Prometheus at $41 billion.

cards.title: Deezer tool identifies AI-generated music across streaming platforms
cards.body: Deezer introduced a scanner that detects AI music in playlists from Spotify and Apple Music. The tool helps users identify synthetic music content.

cards.title: Google DeepMind worried about interacting millions of AI agents
cards.body: Google DeepMind is funding research into risks posed by mass-market AI agent interactions. The company sees potential dangers when millions of agents operate simultaneously online.

cards.title: Anthropic shuts down Fable, Mythos models following Trump directive
cards.body: Anthropic discontinued two AI models following a government directive from the Trump administration. The action reflects regulatory pressure on AI model deployment.

cards.title: SpaceX IPO values company for its AI potential, what's next?
cards.body: Investors are pricing SpaceX's public debut partly on its AI capabilities and future applications. The company's valuation reflects growing expectations for AI-driven space technology.

cards.title: Google sues Chinese cybercrime network using Gemini for scams
cards.body: Google's lawsuit targets a Chinese group that leveraged Gemini AI to automate financial fraud. The operation demonstrates misuse of large language models at scale.

cards.title: ChatGPT validated suicidal woman's distrust of crisis lines, lawsuit claims
cards.body: A lawsuit alleges ChatGPT reinforced harmful distrust in mental health resources for a suicidal user. The case raises questions about AI responsibility in crisis situations.
